data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T232624-7ca9a21a/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T232624-7ca9a21a/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T232624-7ca9a21a/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T232624-7ca9a21a/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T232624-7ca9a21a/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-7fe583dc">
    <title>work: run periodic background cleanup for long-lived workers</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Cleanup cannot rely only on the next ddx try setup path. Long-lived ddx work processes and server-managed workers can sit idle or run many attempts while stale DDx temp worktrees, heartbeat files, and partial setup directories accumulate in the background.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 starts the work loop and emits loop.start/loop.end, but there is no periodic cleanup worker tied to long-lived polling or graceful shutdown.
- cli/cmd/work.go:101 and related command plumbing start ddx work without a cleanup cadence or cleanup lock.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:49 documents server/FEAT-010 worker state, but server-managed worker paths do not share a background cleanup contract.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:476-497 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:232-240 require startup, periodic, shutdown, and background cleanup with jitter and a cleanup lock.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add background cleanup orchestration for long-lived ddx work/server worker processes using the cleanup manager from the base cleanup bead.
- Run cleanup at ddx work startup, periodically while polling, after setup/finalization failures before the next claim, and during graceful signal shutdown.
- Use jitter plus a project cleanup lock so multiple workers do not prune simultaneously.
- Emit structured cleanup events and operator-visible summaries only when cleanup reclaims meaningful resources or blocks progress.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the cleanup manager primitives here.
- Do not replace server-spawn migration work in ddx-eccc6efb; if server workers exec ddx work, they may inherit this behavior instead of duplicating it.
- Do not delete complete evidence or preserved worktrees.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestWorkCleanup_RunsAtStartup verifies ddx work invokes the cleanup manager before the first ready bead claim.
2. TestWorkCleanup_RunsPeriodicallyWhilePolling uses a fake clock/ticker to verify long-lived polling triggers cleanup without busy looping.
3. TestWorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim verifies setup/finalization failure triggers cleanup before any next candidate is claimed.
4. TestWorkCleanup_UsesProjectLock verifies two workers racing cleanup result in one active cleanup pass and one skipped/observed pass, not concurrent deletion.
5. TestWorkCleanup_ShutdownPassRunsOnSignal verifies graceful cancellation performs a final cleanup pass without preventing claim release.
6. Server-managed worker behavior is covered either by inheriting exec ddx work cleanup or by TestServerWorkerCleanup_UsesSharedManager in ./internal/server.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... -run "TestWorkCleanup|TestServerWorkerCleanup" -count=1 passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:server, area:cli, kind:implementation, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, cleanup, background-worker</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-f3dfde0d" spec-id="SD-025" base-rev="618a5d7e34a35c9aaa86491b204a9143fdf036ff" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T232624-7ca9a21a"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="SD-025" path="docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md">Solution Design: Task Execution Lifecycle</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `d65d881dc` (`feat: add background cleanup loop [ddx-7fe583dc]`).

What changed:
- Added the shared cleanup worker and lock orchestration in [cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_loop.go](/tmp/ddx-land-wt-3520433475/cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_loop.go).
- Wired startup, periodic, pre-claim, and shutdown cleanup into [cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go](/tmp/ddx-land-wt-3520433475/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go).
- Passed the shared cleanup manager into `ddx work` and server workers from [cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go](/tmp/ddx-land-wt-3520433475/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go) and [cli/internal/server/workers.go](/tmp/ddx-land-wt-3520433475/cli/internal/server/workers.go).
- Added focused coverage in [cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go](/tmp/ddx-land-wt-3520433475/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go) for startup, periodic, failure-before-next-claim, lock, and shutdown behavior.
- Refreshed the `fizeau` checksum in [cli/go.sum](/tmp/ddx-land-wt-3520433475/cli/go.sum) so the toolchain could verify the module currently being downloaded.

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkCleanup|TestWorkUsesProjectRootForNoWorkScan|TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue|TestDrain_RoutingPreflightRunsOnce|TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits|TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits' -count=1`
- `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/server/... -run 'TestWorkCleanup|TestWorkUsesProjectRootForNoWorkScan|TestWorkDefaultPollIntervalExitsOnEmptyQueue|TestWorkLongRunningPollIntervalRemainsOptIn|TestWorkPassthroughNotValidated|TestServerWorkerCleanup' -count=1`
- `cd cli && CGO_ENABLED=1 lefthook run pre-commit`
